hero.title: By 11 December 2027, every product with digital elements needs a security case.
hero.subtitle: OXOT is an operational-technology compliance specialist. We classify your products, build defensible technical files on IEC 62443, and hold reserved assessment slots with our Conformity Body partners — so you're prepared, not queued.
hero.realityCallout: THE 2026 REALITY — zero Notified Bodies designated · Class I self-assessment closed until harmonised standards are cited (~Q2 2027) · designation takes 12–18 months. The queue forms before the doors open.

items.label: conformity mandatory
items.sub: no CE mark, no EU market

departureBoard.title: The departure board
departureBoard.intro: Three roads reach the CE mark before 11 December 2027 — but their departure windows close at different times. Where the bar turns red, that road no longer arrives on time.

milestones.note: last safe entry to the CB queue

segments.caption: departs after Q1 '27 → arrives after the wall
DEPARTURE WINDOW OPEN — reserved slots via OXOT
ROAD CLOSED — waiting on harmonised standards
the gamble: tight window if standards slip

roadsSplit.title: One start. Three roads. Same destination.
roadsSplit.intro: Phase 1 classifies every product and picks its road. Wrong road = the trip restarts, with the queue longer than when you left. OXOT drives on all three.
roadsSplit.startBadge: START · PHASE 1 — Intake & classification, always OXOT

roads.title: Self-declaration
roads.segment: Default products · Class I with a harmonised standard · 17–32 wks
roads.status: ⚠ CLOSED FOR CLASS I — until standards are cited (~Q2 2027)
roads.body: OXOT runs all three phases on our bench; you sign your own Declaration of Conformity.
roads.oxotDoes: OXOT runs all three phases on our bench; you sign your own DoC.
roads.ceMarkNote: CE MARK · own Declaration of Conformity

roads.title: Type examination
roads.segment: Class I today · all Class II · Critical (until EUCC) · 4–8 mo
roads.status: ⛟ RESERVED SLOT — OXOT CB partnerships skip the public queue; formal handover, clean dossier
roads.body: The Conformity Body examines type and processes; OXOT hands over a clean dossier in the format its bench expects.
roads.oxotDoes: OXOT prepares the dossier in the CB's expected format and hands it over into a reserved slot.
roads.ceMarkNote: CE MARK · EU-type certificate + CB number

roads.title: Full quality assurance
roads.segment: Multi-product portfolios — one audited QMS · 8–14 mo build
roads.status: ⟳ SCALES — new products join the approved QMS in 4–8 weeks
roads.body: The CB audits your full QMS — design, testing, production, vulnerability handling — once, portfolio-wide.
roads.oxotDoes: OXOT builds the QMS + SDL evidence and dry-runs the audit; new products join in 4–8 weeks.

roadsSplit.wrongTurns: Testing before classifying · a stale SBOM · a non-standard dossier = findings loops (4–8 wks each) · the Declaration of Conformity signed too early · a rebrand or modification that goes unnoticed.
roadsSplit.footnote: Not sure which road? That's precisely what the 45-minute intake answers.

personas.title: One regulation. Five buyers. The same engine.
personas.intro: Every position in the OT value chain inherits a different CRA exposure — and buys the same three-phase programme, entered through a segment-specific door. Pick yours; the intake comes prepared for it.

cards.title: Product manufacturers
cards.quote: Every line needs its own SBOM, CVD process, risk assessment and technical file — and nobody in this building can tell me which class each product is even in.

cards.title: OEMs
cards.quote: 40 product families, 3 product-security people. And anything we white-label makes us the manufacturer — whether we built it or not.

cards.title: Integrators
cards.quote: 62443-2-4 shows up in every procurement contract now — and when we customise a vendor's product for a project, nobody can tell me whether we just became its manufacturer.

cards.title: Resellers & distributors
cards.quote: We move thousands of SKUs from dozens of suppliers. Which can we legally sell after December 2027 — and which own-brand lines quietly made us a manufacturer?

cards.title: Owner/operators
cards.quote: Much of our supplier portfolio has an unknown CRA posture, our OT contracts run for decades — and the board wants third-party-validated evidence, not vendor promises.

engine.title: Evidence as data, not documents.
engine.intro: Your product is modelled once on OXOT's cyber digital twin. Every bill of materials is standardised and machine-readable; every risk decision carries a written rationale; every output ships twice — a human-readable report per stakeholder, and a machine-readable file on a DEXPI-extended schema. That one model feeds Phases 2 and 3 without re-work.

boms.label: Cryptography: algorithms, keys, certificates, PQC posture
Cloud services, APIs and remote data processing in scope
Deployment context: services, interfaces, update & support paths

engine.bomsNote: Plus supplier SBOM ingestion — upstream value-stream issues surface in your portfolio view, not in a CB finding.
engine.riskTitle: The risk process — IEC 62443, harmonised for OT

riskSteps.title: ALARP → SL-C
riskSteps.body: Capability levels set by ALARP, with written rationale.

riskSteps.title: SL-A
riskSteps.body: Achieved levels verified on the bench (Phase 2).

riskSteps.title: Portfolio view
riskSteps.body: Dependencies, shared platforms, upstream supplier issues.

engine.riskNote: One size does not fit all. Security levels are engineered per zone and justified — ALARP closes the gap between target and capability with rationale a Conformity Body accepts, not a checklist.
engine.outputsHuman: Human-readable — one report, cut per audience: business · engineering · compliance · procurement · programme management · deployment · legal · leadership.
engine.outputsMachine: Machine-readable — a DEXPI engineering schema, extended by OXOT: classifications, BOMs, risk artifacts, SL rationale — versioned, diffable, consumed directly by Phase 2 and by Conformity Bodies.
engine.outputsNote: A document-based programme rots between releases. A model-based one regenerates: change a component, and the SBOM, risk deltas and technical-file sections update as differentials — not a re-engagement.

retainer.title: One retainer. Three phases. A reserved seat.
retainer.intro: A bucket of specialist hours against a transparent rate card, sized by the intake — typical engagements start around €20K. Hours draw down against the phases; retainer clients hold priority on our bench and our Conformity Body slots.

phases.title: Classify & scope
phases.body: Classification with written rationale, gap assessment, IRA · ZCR · SL-T, testing scope — and your road.

phases.title: Test & evidence
phases.body: The 13 properties and 8 processes, evidenced — on our bench, or a dossier the CB waves through, not sends back.

phases.title: File & lifecycle
phases.body: Annex VII technical file, Declaration of Conformity, PSIRT, 5-year updates, 10-year retention.

reservedSeat.title: CB slots, held for you
reservedSeat.body: Our Phase-1 output arrives in the format the bench expects — less churn, fewer loops, shorter queue.

retainer.digitalTwin: Accelerated throughout by OXOT's digital twin — your product modelled once, SBOMs and evidence regenerated across the lifecycle.

whyOxot.title: Why OXOT wins.
whyOxot.intro: Four reasons the same programme costs less, scales further, and holds capacity when the market can't.

pillars.title: OT-native, not IT-adapted
pillars.body: IEC 62443 is our home standard — the clearest route to presumption of conformity for OT products. Every artifact serves 62443 and CRA at once.

pillars.title: Model-based economics
pillars.body: The digital twin turns compliance from a per-document cost into a per-model asset: releases are differentials, portfolios batch shared evidence, marginal cost falls per product.

pillars.title: Capacity when it's scarce
pillars.body: Reserved CB slots and CB-format handovers — the constraint in 2027 is bench time, and retainer clients hold it.

pillars.title: Recurring by design
pillars.body: CRA obligations run for the product lifecycle — 5-year updates, PSIRT, surveillance. The retainer is the delivery vehicle, from ~€20K, scoped at intake.

intake.eyebrow: The intake — how every engagement starts
intake.title: The wall is fixed. The queue is not.
intake.intro: 45 minutes, free, with an OT compliance specialist. You leave with a live classification read, your likely road (A / B+C / H), and a level-of-effort estimate — a scoped retainer proposal with NDA & MSA within 2 business days. You keep all three answers either way.
intake.formHeading: Where do you sit in the chain?
intake.formSub: 45 minutes, free, with an OT compliance specialist. We come prepared for your position.
intake.promise: You leave the call with: your likely classification, your road to the CE mark, and a level-of-effort estimate. Scoped retainer proposal — with NDA & MSA — within 2 business days.

process.title: What happens when you press the button

steps.title: Vincent contacts you
steps.body: Within 2 business days, with the intake preparation sheet.

steps.title: The 45-minute intake
steps.body: Product, markets, classification, road, level of effort. Free.

steps.title: Scoped proposal
steps.body: Within 2 business days — a retainer proposal, with NDA & MSA.

steps.title: Phase 1 begins
steps.body: And your CB slot is reserved if your road needs one.

finalCta.title: Get ready — get in line.
finalCta.line: The wall is fixed. The queue is not. Book the free 45-minute intake.
